title: Ask HN: Add flag for AI-generated articles
titleZh: 询问 HN：为AI生成的文章添加标记
author: levkk
relevanceReason: 帖子讨论是否应在 Hacker News 上为 AI 生成的文章添加标记功能，涉及 AI 内容识别与社区管理，与 AI 和科技社区相关。
textRawHtml:
Should HN add the ability to flag articles as AI-generated? This doesn't have to act as a regular flag, i.e., it won't de-rank the article; it could just show up as an indicator, allowing others (like myself) who don't like reading AI-generated text, to skip it.

Open questions:

1. Why is the regular voting system not enough?

2. Should HN change in response to the gen AI era? It has been successful not changing fundamentals.
textZhHtml:
HN 是否应该增加将文章标记为 AI 生成的功能？这种标记不必像常规标记那样运作，也就是说，它不会降低文章的排名；它只需作为一个提示标识显示出来，让其他（比如我）不喜欢阅读 AI 生成文本的人能够直接跳过。

未解决的问题：

1. 为什么常规投票系统还不够？

2. HN是否应顺应生成式AI时代做出改变？其成功恰恰在于坚守根本原则而未作改变。
summaryZh: Hacker News 用户提议为 AI 生成的文章添加标记功能，以便不喜欢阅读 AI 内容的用户可以跳过。
讨论还涉及是否应调整投票系统以应对生成式 AI 时代的挑战。
highlightsZh: 提议添加 AI 内容标记功能，提升用户体验。
探讨现有投票系统是否足以应对 AI 内容泛滥的问题。
讨论 Hacker News 是否应因生成式 AI 的兴起而改变其核心机制。

comments.author: dang
comments.textRawHtml:
We don't allow genai text on HN itself - see https://news.ycombinator.com/newsguidelines.html#generated and https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47340079. How to enforce it is a separate question, of course, but the rule exists.

We don't have a similar rule yet about article content but my sense is that the community mostly doesn't want to read it—or, to put it more conservatively, discounts it. This is why we see so many "just show me the prompt" responses, along with others like this: https://news.ycombinator.com/genai-pushback. I built that list so I have something to send to users who email about why their genai articles got flagged.

It's a fascinating arms race right now: the AIs are training on the humans but the human hivemind is also training on the AIs. Readers are developing allergic sensitivities to language that sounds like an LLM produced it. The AIs will adapt to this, but the humans will adapt in turn. Where it ends up is anyone's guess.

For the present, there is an emerging class distinction between writing (and writers) that use genai vs. writing that does not. As soon as the "this sounds like an LLM" allergy kicks in, the writing instantly gets relegated to a low-status bucket in the reader's mind. That doesn't mean it won't still get looked at - but it is now under a stigma.

(I was rather pleased with the originality of this until I remembered pg had come up with "writes and write-nots" in https://paulgraham.com/writes.html. Oh well, it's the point that matters.)

This has the happy flipside that anyone who would like readers to classify their article as high-status rather than low-status can apply the judo move of simply writing it themselves.

Now I need to add the disclaimer that none of this is a dismissal of LLM technology per se. We rely on it heavily, and there's no question that it's useful. The question is how to use it (pg again: https://x.com/paulg/status/2058871512451412457) and whether one should use it on writing that one publishes to other humans.

To turn to OP's questions:

> Should HN add the ability to flag articles as AI-generated? [...] it could just show up as an indicator

Flagging-as-just-an-indicator would be tagging, which we've always resisted adding to HN, but I wouldn't rule it out.

What I do think we'll (finally) add is a "please give a reason why you flagged this post" step, and "because I think it's genai" will be one choice among several (spam, offtopic, mean, etc.)

> Why is the regular voting system not enough?

The regular voting system is never enough. https://hn.algolia.com/?dateRange=all&page=0&prefix=false&so...

> Should HN change in response to the gen AI era?

To this I am tempted to reply with https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48887149 in homage to https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=3742902.
comments.textZhHtml:
我们不允许 HN 本身出现 genai 文本 - 请参阅https://news.ycombinator.com/newsguidelines.html#generated 和 https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47340079。当然，如何执行它是一个单独的问题，但规则是存在的。

我们还没有关于文章内容的类似规则，但我的感觉是，社区大多不想阅读它，或者，更保守地说，不重视它。这就是为什么我们看到如此多的“只需向我显示提示”回复，以及其他类似的回复：https://news.ycombinator.com/genai-pushback。我建立了这个列表，所以我有一些东西可以发送给通过电子邮件说明为什么他们的 genai 文章被标记的用户。

现在这是一场令人着迷的军备竞赛：人工智能正在对人类进行训练，但人类的蜂巢思维也在对人工智能进行训练。读者对听起来像是法学硕士所用的语言产生了过敏。人工智能会适应这一点，但人类也会反过来适应。最终结果如何，谁也说不准。

目前，使用 genai 的写作（和作家）与不使用 genai 的写作之间存在着一种新兴的阶级区别。一旦出现“这听起来像是法学硕士”的过敏症，那么这篇文章就会立即在读者的心目中被降级为低地位的桶。这并不意味着它不会仍然受到关注 - 但它现在处于耻辱之下。

（我对这个原创性相当满意，直到我记得 pg 在 https://paulgraham.com/writes.html 中提出了“写入和不写入”。哦，好吧，这就是重要的一点。）

这有一个令人高兴的反面，任何人都喜欢读者将自己的文章归类为高地位而不是低地位可以应用自己简单地撰写文章的柔道动作。

现在我需要添加免责声明，即这一切都不是对 LLM 技术本身的否定。我们严重依赖它，毫无疑问它很有用。问题是如何使用它（再次：https://x.com/paulg/status/2058871512451412457）以及一个人是否应该在向其他人发布的文章中使用它。

转向OP的问题：

> HN应该添加将文章标记为人工智能生成的功能吗？ [...]它可能只是显示为一个指标

标记为只是一个指标将是标记，我们一直拒绝将其添加到HN，但我不会排除它。

我认为我们（最后）添加的是“请给出标记此帖子的原因”步骤，而“因为我认为它是genai”将是多个选择之一（垃圾邮件、离题、刻薄等）

> 为什么常规投票系统不够？

常规投票系统永远不够。 https://hn.algolia.com/?dateRange=all&page=0&prefix=false&so...

> HN 应该改变以应对新一代人工智能时代吗？

对此，我很想用 https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48887149 来回复，以向https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=3742902。

children.author: grayclhn
children.textRawHtml:
I think classifying it as an allergy or a status thing is a little too glib. I’ve read and reviewed, conservatively, hundreds of AI generated documents for work, and “written”/commissioned a bunch too. My biggest issue is that it’s impossible to engage with and give feedback on an AI written document, because it’s impossible to know whether misconceptions or gaps are because the author doesn’t understand the material deeply enough or the author does but the AI doesn’t and the author’s not proofreading carefully enough. Or if a surprising idea is raised — is it the authors insight, can they elaborate on it, where did it come from, etc?

Hackernews isn’t work, obviously, but “it’s impossible to engage deeper with the material because the author doesn’t really exist” is sort of a problem for a discussion site. If the human coauthor puts in enough work, they can make sure the doc really reflects their views and their understanding, but in my experience that’s much less common.
children.textZhHtml:
我认为将其归类为过敏或状态问题有点太圆滑了。保守地说，我已经阅读和审查了数百份人工智能生成的工作文档，并且还“编写”/委托了很多文档。我最大的问题是，不可能参与人工智能编写的文档并提供反馈，因为无法知道误解或差距是因为作者对材料的理解不够深入，还是作者理解了但人工智能没有理解，而且作者校对得不够仔细。或者，如果提出了一个令人惊讶的想法 - 这是作者的见解吗？他们能否详细说明它，它来自哪里，等等？

Hackernews 显然不起作用，但“不可能更深入地研究材料，因为作者并不真正存在”对于讨论网站来说是一个问题。如果人类合著者投入足够的工作，他们可以确保文档真正反映他们的观点和理解，但根据我的经验，这种情况不太常见。

children.author: tripzilch
children.textRawHtml:
This exactly. Whenever someone shows me their (or someone else's) AI-assisted-coded (vibe coded) thing, I can only ever engage with it on a very superficial level, saying "oh cool".

You can't have a discussion about it, how it was done, because for 80% of cases it's "the AI did it".

I don't think this is that interesting or useful for HN, because indeed it's a discussion site.

It would be different if the vibe coded thing didn't come as a "look what it made and its vibe written README" but instead as a human written blog article about "how I made this thing using AI, what models, prompts and harnesses, how the experience was etc etc etc" --- again that would be something interesting to have a discussion about.

But otherwise what is there to say except "yeah cool that's cool that the AI made you that thing"

> it’s impossible to know whether misconceptions or gaps are because the author doesn’t understand the material deeply enough or the author does but the AI doesn’t and the author’s not proofreading carefully enough. Or if a surprising idea is raised — is it the authors insight, can they elaborate on it, where did it come from, etc?
children.textZhHtml:
正是如此。每当有人向我展示他们（或其他人）的人工智能辅助编码（振动编码）的东西时，我只能在非常肤浅的层面上参与其中，说“哦酷”。

你不能讨论它，它是如何完成的，因为在 80% 的情况下，它是“人工智能做到了”。

我认为这对 HN 来说没有那么有趣或有用，因为这确实是一个讨论网站。

如果氛围编码的东西不是“看看它做了什么，它的氛围写自述文件”，而是作为一篇人类撰写的博客文章，内容是“我如何使用人工智能制作这个东西，什么模型，提示和利用，体验如何等等”，那将会有所不同——再次这将是一个有趣的讨论。

但除此之外，除了“是的，很酷，人工智能制作的很酷”之外，还有什么可说的？你那个东西”

>不可能知道误解或差距是因为作者对材料的理解不够深入还是作者理解了但人工智能没有理解并且作者校对得不够仔细。或者，如果提出了一个令人惊讶的想法 - 这是作者的见解吗？他们能否详细说明它，它来自哪里等等？

children.author: mghackerlady
children.textRawHtml: This is becoming a problem on the anti-HN (reddit). Someone will submit something cool, not disclose its status as vibecoded, and then not be able to answer any real questions about whatever it is they submitted. I don't have a problem with vibe coded software in general, but I think sharing it like that is strange. What do these people get from it? Prestige? I don't think they get any, as it's almost always clocked as vibecoded a few minutes after seeing it
children.textZhHtml: 这正在成为反HN (reddit) 上的一个问题。有人会提交一些很酷的东西，但不会透露其振动编码状态，然后无法回答有关他们提交的内容的任何真正问题。一般来说，我对振动编码软件没有任何问题，但我认为这样分享它很奇怪。这些人从中得到什么？声望？我认为他们没有得到任何东西，因为它几乎总是在看到它几分钟后被标记为振动编码

children.author: uncivilized
children.textRawHtml: This occurred on HN as well. It's not just Reddit-coded
children.textZhHtml: 这种情况在HN上也发生过。这并非只是Reddit特有的调性

children.author: westurner
children.textRawHtml:
> what is there to say

Ways to contribute: Peer review, human code review, money for tokens for code and code review, configure SAST and DAST tools to make it DevSecOps instead of DevOpsSec, configure AGENTS.md and an /upgrade-dependencies-and-run-tests skill,

Compare "It's inadequate because it's AI" with "it looks like this generation of agents doesn't yet handle this quality aspect with or without explicit prompts" with "you could improve quality by writing tests, docs, and before that refactor for maintainability and subjective elegance" (if you're trying develop a hit open source project)

> impossible to know whether misconceptions or gaps are because the author doesn’t understand the material

Shouldn't a researcher consider the argument - it's premises and form - instead of tangential ad hominem about the author?

Pushing for well-formed arguments is older than AI
children.textZhHtml:
> 有什么可说的

贡献方式：同行评审、人工代码评审、为代码和代码评审提供代币资金、配置 SAST 和 DAST 工具以使其成为 DevSecOps 而不是 DevOpsSec、配置 AGENTS.md 和 /upgrade-dependency-and-run-tests 技能，

将“它不够充分，因为它是 AI”与“它”进行比较看起来这一代代理还没有在有或没有明确提示的情况下处理这个质量方面”，“你可以通过编写测试、文档以及在可维护性和主观优雅性方面进行重构来提高质量”（如果你正在尝试开发一个热门的开源项目）

> 不可能知道误解或差距是否是因为作者不理解材料

研究人员不应该考虑这个论点 - 这是前提和形式 - 而不是关于作者的切题人身攻击？

推动格式良好的论点比人工智能更古老

children.author: grayclhn
children.textRawHtml:
Just skimmed your resume and, assuming it’s up to date, it looks like you mostly work independently. If that’s the case, I can totally see why my argument doesn’t resonate.

“The author doesn’t understand the material” isn’t an ad hominem — most of my work involves working with other people, usually less experienced, to help them deliver reasonably complex econometric or ML models. Sometimes I’m hands on, usually not. Knowing if it’s “the author not understanding the material” is important because my job is to help them understand it so that they can deliver something that 1) works and 2) does what it’s supposed to. AI written docs are at best a mixed blessing.

Or maybe it’s me not understanding! Either way, if something looks wrong, I want to discuss it with people who know the subject matter and can talk me through the parts that look off so that I can learn from it.
children.textZhHtml:
刚刚浏览了您的简历，假设它是最新的，看起来您大部分时间都是独立工作。如果是这样的话，我完全明白为什么我的论点没有引起共鸣。

“作者不理解材料”并不是人身攻击——我的大部分工作涉及与其他人（通常经验不足）合作，帮助他们提供相当复杂的计量经济学或机器学习模型。有时我会亲自动手，但通常不会。知道是否是“作者不理解材料”很重要，因为我的工作是帮助他们理解材料，以便他们能够交付 1）有效且 2）达到预期效果的东西。 AI 编写的文档充其量是喜忧参半。

或者也许是我不理解！不管怎样，如果有什么地方看起来不对劲，我想和那些了解主题的人讨论一下，并且可以告诉我那些看起来不对劲的部分，这样我就可以从中学习。

children.author: grayclhn
children.textRawHtml: Too late to edit - should have looked at comment history and not profile I guess, because like an idiot I spent time replying to a clearly ai-written comment… Well GP isn’t going to learn anything from my (gracious! beautiful! dare I say, heroic?) attempt to find a common shared experience in our otherwise separate lives, but hopefully other people got something out of it. I hope to god there’s a lesson there for someone.
children.textZhHtml: 编辑太晚了——我想应该查看评论历史而不是个人资料，因为我像个白痴一样花时间回复一条清晰的人工智能评论……好吧，GP不会从我（仁慈！美丽！我敢说，英雄？）尝试在我们各自独立的生活中找到共同的经历中学到任何东西，但希望其他人能从中得到一些东西。我希望上帝能给某人一个教训。

children.author: westurner
children.textRawHtml:
What?

No, that's not an ai written comment either.
children.textZhHtml:
什么？

不，这也不是人工智能撰写的评论。

children.author: westurner
children.textRawHtml:
There will always be a place for handmade furniture.

AI is not useful for econometric ML because the client doesn't understand the model?

"Explain the model to the client like they're five and they don't remember our fee is already negotiated" has inaccurate model replies in your field?

Even before LLM AI, could an OpenCog dog have done the model search and optimization?

What is the difference between AutoML's predictive error next to expert-biased model selection and parameter grid search with CV in your field this year?

How could AI help validate - you mentioned econometrics - econometrics latex in manuscripts?

- Port manuscripts to interactive notebooks

- translate LaTeX to a CAS syntax (e.g. SymPy), test to validate, and enqueue for human review

- Develop causal experiments based upon observational data from non-blinded experiments, like this: > "Answering causal questions using observational data" (2021) [PDF] https://www.nobelprize.org/uploads/2021/10/advanced-economic... ... 2021 Nobel Prize in Economics: https://www.nobelprize.org/prizes/economic-sciences/2021/pop...

Dismayed at AI bashing because its consistently winning at red team and blue team tasks and now better than preliminary code review and if the hatemongering bandwagon wins our team will lose the ai advantage.

I'm looking at seriously billions in cost reductions and sustainability wins from LLMs applied to production processes and products (in various non-tech and tech fields). Justify estimates of CapEx, OpEx, Marginal cost for Garage, Lab, Pilot, Production, and Giga-Production scales. Find synergies, valorize waste, eliminate hazards, eliminate external costs.

But that's all only exploratory, discovery phase work.

Does a persin need to rephrase it by human proxy in order to link to it, if they've applied AI?

There is significant real potential opportunity to applied AI, so it's disheartening to learn that the AI spam justifies new censorship controls in this also an election year.

It's really frustrating and alienating to be subject to false allegations. It's really frustrating to be censored or shawbdned.

When it's "censor that and all their other s because it's AI" but it's not. No AI ever should not be the TOS.

A person could create a "split with us against AI" site as a separate forum and test and buy insurance for that.

HN does not but Reddit supports bots; you put "bot" in the bot's username.
children.textZhHtml:
手工家具永远有一席之地。

人工智能对于计量经济机器学习没有用，因为客户不理解模型？

“向客户解释模型，就像他们五岁一样，他们不记得我们的费用已经协商好了”在你的领域有不准确的模型回复吗？

即使在 LLM AI 之前，OpenCog 狗是否可以完成模型搜索和模型搜索？优化？

今年在您所在领域中，AutoML 的预测误差与专家偏向模型选择和 CV 参数网格搜索之间有什么区别？

AI 如何帮助验证 - 您提到了计量经济学 - 手稿中的计量经济学乳胶？

- 将手稿移植到交互式笔记本

- 将 LaTeX 翻译为 CAS 语法（例如 SymPy），进行测试以验证，以及排队等待人工审核

- 根据非盲实验的观察数据开发因果实验，如下所示：>“使用观察数据回答因果问题”(2021) [PDF] https://www.nobelprize.org/uploads/2021/10/advanced-economic... ... 2021 年诺贝尔经济学奖： https://www.nobelprize.org/prizes/economic-sciences/2021/pop...

对人工智能的抨击感到沮丧，因为它在红队和蓝队任务中一直获胜，现在比初步代码审查要好，如果煽动仇恨的浪潮获胜，我们的团队将失去人工智能优势。

我正在认真考虑将法学硕士应用于生产流程和产品（在各种非技术和技术领域）带来的数十亿美元的成本削减和可持续发展胜利字段）。证明对车库、实验室、试点、生产和千兆生产规模的资本支出、运营支出和边际成本的估计。找到协同效应，优化浪费，消除危险，消除外部成本。

但这只是探索性、发现阶段的工作。

如果应用了人工智能，人员是否需要通过人类代理重新措辞才能链接到它？

应用人工智能确实存在巨大的潜在机会，因此令人沮丧的是，人工智能垃圾邮件证明了新的审查控制措施是合理的。同样是选举年。

受到虚假指控确实令人沮丧和疏远。被审查或被审查真的很令人沮丧。

当它是“审查那个和所有其他的，因为它是人工智能”但事实并非如此。 任何人工智能都不应该成为服务条款。

一个人可以创建一个“与我们分裂反对人工智能”网站作为单独的论坛，并为此进行测试和购买保险。

HN 不这样做，但 Reddit 支持机器人；您将“bot”放入机器人的用户名中。

children.author: thevinter
children.textRawHtml:
>My biggest issue is that it’s impossible to engage with and give feedback on an AI written document

Thanks for giving shape to a general annoyance, in my case with code, that I only recently started noticing.

When discussing code with my colleagues, especially with those that I do not know particularly well, I often relied on the quality of the code they produced to modulate how technical the conversation ought to be be.

Now, instead, I often see very complex code from people that I know wouldn't have been able to produce it themselves, and I have no clue how to engage with it, how to review it with them, how detailed can my comments be etc.

It's pretty annoying.
children.textZhHtml:
>我最大的问题是不可能参与人工智能编写的文档并提供反馈

感谢您让我最近才开始注意到的普遍烦恼（就我而言，代码而言）。

与同事讨论代码时，尤其是与那些我不太了解的人讨论代码时，我经常依靠他们生成的代码的质量来调整对话的技术性

现在，我经常看到那些我知道自己无法编写的人编写的非常复杂的代码，而且我不知道如何编写参与其中，如何与他们一起审查，我的评论可以多详细等等。

这很烦人。

children.author: woolion
children.textRawHtml:
>My biggest issue is that it’s impossible to engage with and give feedback on an AI written document, because it’s impossible to know whether misconceptions or gaps are because the author doesn’t understand the material deeply enough or the author does but the AI doesn’t and the author’s not proofreading carefully enough.

FWIW, same problem with PRs or PoC that I have to work on; now my first question is, "did you know about his behaviour?". The first step, getting a decent spec, is delayed to after a first draft implementation is already pushed...
children.textZhHtml:
>我最大的问题是不可能参与人工智能编写的文档并提供反馈，因为无法知道误解或差距是因为作者对材料理解不够深入，还是作者理解但人工智能没有理解，而且作者校对不够仔细。

FWIW，我必须解决 PR 或 PoC 的同样问题；现在我的第一个问题是，“你知道他的行为吗？”。第一步，获得一个像样的规范，被推迟到第一稿实施已经推送之后......

children.author: tlb
children.textRawHtml:
You can engage with an AI-written document! Simply paste the document into the AI (same or different as the AI that wrote it) and ask questions. You can get an answer in seconds compared to an hour (if it's a blog post by an HN reader), days (if it's a professional blogger), or never (if it's by a journalist).

Perhaps AI-written articles should include the prompt as hidden text on the page. Or include a link with the prompt embedded, that causes the AI to spit out the same article that you could then ask questions about.
children.textZhHtml:
您可以参与人工智能编写的文档！只需将文档粘贴到人工智能（与编写该文档的人工智能相同或不同）并提出问题即可。您可以在几秒钟内得到答案，而不是一小时（如果是 HN 读者的博客文章）、几天（如果是专业博主）或永远不会（如果是记者）。

也许人工智能撰写的文章应该将提示作为隐藏文本包含在页面上。或者包含一个嵌入提示的链接，这会导致人工智能吐出同一篇文章，然后您可以提出问题。

children.author: grayclhn
children.textRawHtml:
I can indeed use AI to do research on topics I learn about on HN. That’s a different activity than what I come to HN for, or when I’m reviewing a proposal for something at work, or if I’m interested in an article and want to learn more about some of the details that didn’t make the final draft from either the author or from other people with experience in the area.

“Why didn’t the author consider [this thing that seems similar]” is almost never something you can ask an llm, especially for articles where the author is putting in so little of their own effort that they don’t edit out obvious AI tells.
children.textZhHtml:
我确实可以使用人工智能来研究我在 HN 上学到的主题。这与我来 HN 的目的不同，或者当我正在审查工作中的某项提案时，或者如果我对一篇文章感兴趣并希望从作者或其他在该领域有经验的人那里了解更多未形成最终草案的细节。

“为什么作者没有考虑[这个看起来相似的事情]”几乎从来不是你可以问法学硕士的问题，尤其是对于作者投入如此少的内容的文章。他们自己的努力，他们没有编辑掉明显的人工智能告诉。

children.author: baubino
children.textRawHtml:
> it’s impossible to know whether misconceptions or gaps are because the author doesn’t understand the material deeply enough or the author does but the AI doesn’t and the author’s not proofreading carefully enough

It doesn’t matter. The human responsible for the written piece is … responsible for the written piece. If someone is willing to use AI to supplement their own intellect and/or writing abilities, then they need to take ownership of the mistakes, misconceptions, etc., that come along with that, the same as an author who does all the work themselves. The problem you’ve identified here is that the human responsible for the writing is not fully taking responsibility for the writing.
children.textZhHtml:
> 我们无从得知，文中的误解或疏漏究竟是因为作者对材料理解不够深入，还是作者懂了但AI没懂，且作者没有仔细校对

这并不重要。对文章负责的人……就是要对文章负责。如果有人愿意使用AI来弥补自身智力或写作能力的不足，那么他们就需要为随之而来的错误、误解等承担责任，就像完全独立完成作品的作者一样。你在这里指出的问题是，对文章负责的人并没有完全承担起对文章的责任。

children.author: dang
children.textRawHtml: That's interesting, thank you! My only pushback is that my bit about "allergy" and "status" is about the audience response here, whereas you seem to be talking on a somewhat different level.
children.textZhHtml: 这很有趣，谢谢！我唯一的反对意见是，我关于“过敏”和“状态”的部分是关于观众的反应，而你似乎在某种程度上不同的层面上谈论。

children.author: grayclhn
children.textRawHtml: Oh for sure. My only point is that IME (and I’d assume many other commenters) the quality of discussion and engagement around an imperfect ai-written doc is considerably worse than the same doc written by a human. Work is where it’s most obvious to me… because many of these are design docs for systems I care deeply about us delivering, but I’ve noticed the same thing elsewhere.
children.textZhHtml: 哦，当然。我唯一的观点是，IME（我假设还有许多其他评论者）围绕不完美的人工智能编写的文档进行讨论和参与的质量比人类编写的同一篇文档要差得多。工作对我来说是最明显的……因为其中许多是我非常关心我们交付的系统的设计文档，但我在其他地方也注意到了同样的事情。

children.author: tripzilch
children.textRawHtml:
But you wouldn't have called it "allergy" or a "status" problem when considering the audience's response to human written posts of a similar low quality level.

It's always been the case that the HN audience has been "allergic" to low effort slop (including human generated).

The OP (and my) point was trying to point out how the AI generated stuff might masquerade as something "interesting" if it had been human written, but actually is much of a nothing burger when there's no human behind it:

if a human made a strange, weird, or even sub-optimal design decision, that's something to possibly have an interesting discussion about.

if an AI does the same, first of, I don't care and don't wanna guess why its weights did the thing it did, it's just not as interesting as human motivations, UNLESS the main meat of the article/link is in fact about (again the human experience of) "how I made this thing, using AI, what prompts, harness, models, etc".

I hope it makes sense and allows you to no longer dismiss this as an "allergy" (aka the audience's problem), but as something that is actually a quality problem with the post.
children.textZhHtml:
但是，当考虑到受众对类似低质量水平的人类书面帖子的反应时，你不会将其称为“过敏”或“状态”问题。

HN 受众一直对低努力的废液（包括人类生成的）“过敏”。

OP（和我的）观点试图指出人工智能生成的东西可能如何伪装成如果它是人类写的，那么它会是“有趣”的东西，但实际上，当没有人类在背后支持时，它就毫无意义了：

如果一个人类做出了一个奇怪的、怪异的、甚至是次优的设计决策，那么这可能是一个有趣的讨论。

如果一个人工智能做了同样的事情，首先，我不关心也不想猜测为什么它的权重会做它所做的事情，它只是不像人类的动机那么有趣，除非文章/链接的主要内容实际上是关于（又是人类的经验）“我如何制作这个东西，使用人工智能，什么提示，利用，模型等”。

我希望它有意义，并让您不再将其视为“过敏”（又名观众的问题），而是实际上是帖子的质量问题。

children.author: heurist
children.textRawHtml: You are heavily discounting the human side of the generation effort. The first output from an AI might be slop but once you start injecting your own ideas and desires it lands much further away from what any AI would output on its own. (Not that there's no zero-effort slop around - there's a lot).
children.textZhHtml: 您严重低估了一代人的努力。人工智能的第一个输出可能是草率的，但一旦你开始注入自己的想法和愿望，它就会与任何人工智能自己输出的结果相去甚远。 （并不是说没有零努力的浪费 - 有很多）。

children.author: tripzilch
children.textRawHtml:
Sorry if I made it seem I think the human side of AI-use is without effort, because it's not and can take a lot of time (and effort/energy).

It is, however, my experience that even the people who spend a lot of time and effort generating something with AI, tend to shrug when you ask a deeper question about the project, and often point to the AI generated documentation, which is, let me be subtle and say, "not really a joy to read", and especially pales in comparison to having a conversation with a human who spent their effort on learning how to do the thing it did, instead of prompting an AI.

I'm also not saying there aren't any exceptions to this. But I've found them rare.

Like I said, even if the human doesn't quite understand what they generated, even then it would be preferable to read a human written article about how it was generated, using what tools and harnesses, etc.

I come to this website to learn stuff and converse with other people, not just to go "ooh aah" at what somebody generated with AI (if that is all they can offer).

Hope that makes sense.
children.textZhHtml:
很抱歉，我认为人工智能的人性化使用是不需要付出努力的，因为它不是而且可能需要大量时间（和努力/精力）。

然而，根据我的经验，即使是那些花费大量时间和精力用人工智能生成某些东西的人，当你询问有关该项目的更深层次问题时，往往会耸耸肩，并经常指向人工智能生成的文档，也就是说，让我微妙地说，“这并不是一种真正的乐趣”。阅读”，与与人类对话相比尤其苍白，人类花费精力学习如何做它所做的事情，而不是提示人工智能。

我也不是说这没有任何例外。但我发现它们很少见。

就像我说的，即使人类不太理解它们生成的内容，即便如此，最好还是阅读一篇人类撰写的文章，了解它是如何生成的，使用什么工具和工具等。

我来到这个网站是为了学习东西并与其他人交谈，而不仅仅是为了对某人用人工智能生成的内容感到“哦啊”（如果这就是他们能做的）提供）。

希望这是有道理的。
